transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Thanks for calling Meridian Health Line, this is Ava. What's going on today?

transcript.speaker: patient
transcript.text: Hi, um, I've had a really bad headache since last night and now I feel a little dizzy.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: I'm sorry you're feeling this way. Can you tell me if you've had any vision changes, numbness, or trouble speaking?

transcript.speaker: patient
transcript.text: No, none of that. Just the headache and some dizziness when I stand up too fast.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Okay, that's helpful. Have you had any fever, neck stiffness, or recent injury to your head?

transcript.speaker: patient
transcript.text: No fever or injury. I did skip lunch and dinner yesterday though.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: That could definitely be contributing. Given no red-flag symptoms, this sounds like it could be dehydration or low blood sugar rather than an emergency.

transcript.speaker: patient
transcript.text: That's a relief. So what should I do?

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Drink some water, eat a small meal, and rest for a bit. If the headache doesn't improve in a few hours or you develop new symptoms, please seek urgent care.

transcript.speaker: patient
transcript.text: Okay, I can do that. Should I still see my regular doctor?

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Yes, I'd recommend a routine visit with Dr. Alvarez this week just to check in. I can help schedule that now if you'd like.

transcript.speaker: patient
transcript.text: Yes please, that would be great. Thank you.
